random_top_expectation.E[rank]: 127 - sum_{k>=1} P(val>=k) ≈ 127 - 1 = 126 for nonzero; incl zero tiny

three_core_product: Each R-core = hash_to_fp(toep(top,y)); independent tops per domain. Even if one map has rank 126, product R1*R2*R3 in Fp still looks uniform nonzero; no public core outputs to enforce linear constraints on y.
close_reason: Rank follows random-top expectation (mean ~126, full-rank rate ~50%); degeneracy is valuation-driven, not a stable secret-independent kernel; no public R-core; three-core product erases usable linear constraints.
applicability_to_active: Active tops are AES-CTR keyed by prf_k — unknown. Map degeneracy cannot be exploited without top. QP-02 already proved only 127 bits of y matter; RP-04 shows those 127 bits are essentially full-rank into the 127-bit toep output.
